success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter mode was feasible (import feasibility 0.83, low intrusiveness risk), enabling broad automatic endpoint exposure.
Generated MCP plugin started successfully and passed health checks (stdio transport, service_health=healthy).
Repository has clear Python package structure and rich functional surface area, which helped tool generation.
failure_reasons: DeepWiki analysis failed (non-blocking) and reduced external context enrichment.
Original project tests were not validated as passing in this run (passed=false with empty details), creating uncertainty about baseline correctness.

node_performance.download_time: Completed successfully via zip fallback; likely efficient for a medium repository (113 files).
node_performance.analysis_time: Moderate-to-high due to broad AST/module discovery and endpoint extraction across many packages.
node_performance.generation_time: Efficient; core MCP scaffold and adapter files were generated without reported errors.
node_performance.test_time: MCP plugin smoke/health tests succeeded quickly; original project test execution appears incomplete or not instrumented.

resource_usage.memory_efficiency: Insufficient telemetry (reported 0 MB), but no OOM or instability signs during generation/run.
resource_usage.cpu_efficiency: Insufficient telemetry (reported 0%), execution duration suggests acceptable CPU use for medium complexity.
resource_usage.disk_usage: Generated artifact set is small; repository contains sizable static JSON map/data assets that dominate storage footprint.
